title: Interval Timing Task
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 139 participants (94 with Parkinson's disease and 45 healthy controls) performing an interval timing task. Participants were instructed to judge the passage of either 3-second or 7-second intervals while 64-channel EEG was recorded. The task involved visual cues and keyboard responses, with distractors presented to prevent explicit counting strategies. This dataset provides neurophysiological data for investigating temporal perception and motor timing deficits in Parkinson's disease.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a 64-channel BrainVision cap during an interval timing task. Participants viewed instructional text indicating trial duration (3 or 7 seconds) followed by a visual cue (solid box) marking interval onset. Participants responded via spacebar when they judged the interval to have elapsed. Random distractor vowels were presented during trials to discourage counting strategies.
